rationale: At 2,337 lines this spec sits 163 lines below the 2,500-line "RECOMMENDED" threshold, but its reference-usage context pushes the effective pressure well past that threshold: an LLM consuming DDIS to write a new spec must hold ~2,337 lines of reference plus ~2,000–3,000 lines of output simultaneously, totalling 4,300–5,300 lines of context — a clear budget problem. The modularization overhead is justified because the five proposed modules are genuinely independent reference surfaces: an LLM writing invariants for a new spec only needs the core-framework module; one decomposing a large spec needs only the modularization-protocol module. Two-tier mode is correct because the system constitution (all 16 invariant declarations + 7 ADR decisions + glossary stubs + quality-gate summaries) fits comfortably within 200 lines, well under the 400-line two-tier ceiling.
usage_context_analysis: This spec is a meta-standard consumed as a reference alongside other work, not a standalone implementation target. An LLM reads DDIS while simultaneously producing a new conforming specification — meaning it must hold the full reference in context while generating 2,000–3,000 lines of output. This is the highest-pressure usage pattern the decision framework recognises. A standalone reader who only needs to understand DDIS (not apply it concurrently) could manage the monolith; any LLM actively writing a new spec cannot. Modularization allows the LLM to load only the relevant slice of DDIS for the task at hand (e.g., just the element-specifications module when drafting PART II content).

proposed_domains.name: spec-science
proposed_domains.description: Foundational theory, constraints, and invariants — what a DDIS spec must BE. Covers the formal model, all base invariants (INV-001–INV-010), ADRs 001–005, quality gates 1–6, non-negotiables, non-goals, and the PART I formal model.

proposed_domains.name: modularization
proposed_domains.description: The complete scaling protocol for specs that exceed context budgets. Covers §0.13 in full: tiered constitution, invariants INV-011–INV-016, ADRs 006–007, quality gates M-1–M-5, manifest schema, assembly rules, consistency checks, cascade protocol, and migration procedure.

proposed_domains.name: element-reference
proposed_domains.description: Per-element specification: what each DDIS structural element must contain, with quality criteria, anti-patterns, and cross-reference requirements. Covers PART II Chapters 2–7.

proposed_domains.name: practice
proposed_domains.description: How to use DDIS in practice: voice and style, proportional weight, cross-reference patterns, authoring sequence, common mistakes, validation procedures, evolution guidance, risk register, and the master TODO.

proposed_modules.name: system-constitution
sections: Preamble (design goal, core promise, document note, how-to-use)

proposed_modules.name: core-framework
sections: PART I Chapter 1 (Formal Model, State Machine, Complexity)

proposed_modules.name: guidance-and-practice
sections: Chapter 8: Voice and Style (§8.1–§8.3 Anti-Pattern Catalog)
Chapter 11: Applying DDIS (§11.1 Authoring Sequence, §11.2 Common Mistakes)
Chapter 12: Validating a DDIS Spec (§12.1 Self-Validation, §12.2 External Validation)
Chapter 13: Evolving a DDIS Spec (§13.1 Living Spec, §13.2 Versioning)
